simple_types.name: Bitmap_Color_Mode
simple_types.code:
type Bitmap_Color_Mode is
  (ARGB_8888,
   RGB_888,
   RGB_565,
   ARGB_1555,
   ARGB_4444,
   L_8,
   AL_44,
   AL_88,
   …,
   M_1
  ) with Size => 4;

array_types.name: Point_Array
array_types.code: type Point_Array is array (Natural range <>) of Point;

record_types.name: Bitmap_Color
Point
Rect

access_types.name: Any_Bitmap_Buffer
access_types.code: type Any_Bitmap_Buffer is access all Bitmap_Buffer'Class;

subprograms.name: Color_Mode
subprograms.code: function Color_Mode (Buffer : Bitmap_Buffer) return Bitmap_Color_Mode

documentation.description:
The buffer color mode. Note that not all color modes are supported by
the hardware acceleration (if any), so you need to check your actual
hardware to optimize buffer transfers.

Cubic_Bezier
Draw_Circle
Draw_Horizontal_Line
Draw_Line

documentation.description:
If fast is set, then the line thickness uses squares to draw, while
if not set, then the line will be composed of circles, much slower to
draw but providing nicer line cap.

documentation.description:
Height of the buffer. Note that it's the user-visible height
(see below for the meaning of the Swapped value).

subprograms.name: Mapped_In_RAM
subprograms.code: function Mapped_In_RAM (Buffer : Bitmap_Buffer) return Boolean

documentation.description: Return True is the bitmap is storred in the CPU address space

subprograms.name: Memory_Address
subprograms.code: function Memory_Address (Buffer : Bitmap_Buffer) return System.Address

documentation.description:
Return the address of the bitmap in the CPU address space. If the bitmap
is not in the CPU address space, the result is undefined.

subprograms.name: Swapped
subprograms.code: function Swapped (Buffer : Bitmap_Buffer) return Boolean

documentation.description:
If Swapped return True, operations on this buffer will consider:
Width0 = Height
Height0 = Width
Y0 = Buffer.Width - X - 1
X0 = Y

As an example, the Bitmap buffer that corresponds to a 240x320
swapped display (to display images in landscape mode) with have
the following values:
Width => 320
Height => 240
Swapped => True
So Put_Pixel (Buffer, 30, 10, Color) will place the pixel at
Y0 = 320 - 30 - 1 = 289
X0 = 10

subprograms.name: Width
subprograms.code: function Width (Buffer : Bitmap_Buffer) return Natural

documentation.description:
Width of the buffer. Note that it's the user-visible width
(see below for the meaning of the Swapped value).
